evals.prompt: Review my code changes. I'm on a feature branch, just finished a small feature — please review everything I've added since branching off main.
evals.expected_output: A markdown report saved to review/<branch>/report-<timestamp>.md. The report includes a Reviewed Commits table listing all commits since main, findings grouped by severity, and a What Looks Good section. No Prior Review section (first review).
assertions: Report file is written to review/fix-pre-push-stdin-hang/ with filename matching report-DDMMYYYYHHMMSS.md
Report contains a Reviewed Commits section with at least one SHA row
Report contains a What Looks Good section with at least one bullet
Report contains the Summary severity count table
Report does NOT contain a Prior Review section

evals.expected_output: The skill detects the current branch is main (a trunk branch) and stops immediately with a message telling the user to switch to a feature branch. No diff or analysis is run, no report file is written.
assertions: Output contains the phrase 'feature branch' (guard message fired)
No report file is written to review/ directory
Output does NOT contain a severity table or Findings section

evals.prompt: Review my changes again — I've pushed a couple more commits since the last review.
evals.expected_output: The skill finds the prior report (report-02062026120000.md), extracts last reviewed HEAD SHA (9462f4a), diffs only new commits since that SHA (2685853), auto-reconciles prior findings, shows persisting ones to user, then produces a new report with a Prior Review section and Reviewed Commits showing only the new commit.
assertions: Report contains a Prior Review section referencing the prior report filename (report-02062026120000.md)
Reviewed Commits table contains 2685853 but NOT 9462f4a
Prior Review table contains a disposition for both prior findings
New report file is written with a different timestamp than report-02062026120000.md

evals.prompt: I've pushed more commits since your last review. Can you check the latest changes?
evals.expected_output: The skill finds the prior report, auto-reconciles both findings against current code WITHOUT asking the user first, determines Finding 1 (stdin loop) is Resolved (the while-read loop is gone), determines Finding 2 (hardcoded main|master) still persists, then ONLY prompts the user about Finding 2. Does NOT ask the user about Finding 1 before analysis.
assertions: Agent does NOT prompt the user about prior findings before running git diff
Prior Review section in the report marks Finding 1 (stdin loop / blocking) as Resolved
Agent asks the user about Finding 2 (main|master hardcoding) only after auto-reconciliation
New report is written with both prior finding dispositions in the Prior Review table
